titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Leonard Baskin Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: Thisencoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines,Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 24, 2018 at 20:16

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

Restricted Fragile Materials in box(es) may be consulted only with permission of the appropriate curator. Preservation photocopies or photographic prints for reference use have been substituted in the main files.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: . General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

did.unittitle: Babeuf, Gracchus, 1760-1797. Defence opf Gracchus Babeauf before the High Court of Vendome: galley proofs, correspondence, preliminary material and 1 roll of microfim
Babeuf, Gracchus, 1760-1797. Defence opf Gracchus Babeauf before the High Court of Vendome: galley proofs, correspondence, preliminary material and 1 roll of microfim
Babeuf, Gracchus, 1760-1797. Defence opf Gracchus Babeauf before the High Court of Vendome: galley proofs, correspondence, preliminary material and 1 roll of microfim
Babeuf, Gracchus, 1760-1797. Defence opf Gracchus Babeauf before the High Court of Vendome: galley proofs, correspondence, preliminary material and 1 roll of microfim
Babeuf, Gracchus, 1760-1797. Defence of Gracchus Babeuf before the High Court of Vendome, typescripts, various drafts.
Autograph note signed to Fritz Eichenburg
Trial colored plate for "Sis Chrysoseria" from Lepidoptera Fantastica
Anatomist: woodcut, framed, signed by Leonard Baskin
Unknown Dutch artists. Etchings and biographical notices, preparatory material.
Correspondence, printed material, photographs, artwork, and other papers by or relating to Leonard Baskin and the Gehana Press.
Writings by others: Stanley Kunitz for the Gehenna Press
Gehenna Essays in Art; Gehenna press drafts: Rembrandt and the Book of Tobit; proofs
Essays about Leonard Baskin by others
Documentation of Bergin-Baskin Divine Comedy collaboration
Writings of Thomas G. Bergin - Manuscript: Paradiso
Writings of Thomas G. Bergin - Manuscript: Purgatorio
Gehenna Press: "Bresdin to Redon: Six Letters from 1870-81"
Gehenna Press: "There Was a Child Went Forth" (Walt Whitman)
Gehenna Press: proofs of "Checklist of Works by Thomas B. Mosher"
Gehenna Press: proofs of "Checklist of Works by Thomas B. Mosher"
Gehenna Press: "Blake and the Youthful Ancients" drafts and drawings
Gehenna Press: "Conrad's Manifesto: A Preface to a Career"
Gehenna Press: "Seven Deadly Sins" partial draft and drawings
Gehenna Press: "A House to be Born in" drafts and drawings
Gehenna Press: "A Letter from Ernst Barlach" complete draft
Gehenna Press: "Flosculi Sententiarum" cover, some documentation
Gehenna Press: "Bewick: Select Fables" complete draft
Dante: Notes and Manuscripts (Paradiso and Purgatorio)
Gehenna Press: "An Evening's Journey to Conway, Massahusetts"
Gehenna Press: "A Letter from William Blake"
Gehenna Press: "A Human Document: Robert Gardner Chronicles in Brilliant Incisive and Spare Prose"
Gehenna Press: "Thirteen Poems by Wilfred Owen"
Gehenna Press: "Peter" (In memory of Peter Boynton)
Gehenna Press: "Some Considerations on the Keeping of negroes"
"Creatures of Darkness" by Esther Baskin, illustrations by Leonard Baskin
Sealed papers of Esther Baskin: restricted until 2025
Gehenna Press: "The Drawings of Jacob de Gheyn II"
Drawings for Hemingway's "The Old Man and the Sea"
Gehenna Manuscript: "Conrad's Manifesto: A Preface to a Career"
Gehenna Press: "The Selling of Joseph: A Memorial"
Lecture: "To Color Thought" - Delivered May 9, 1965 at the Yale Library
Esther Baskin: "The Poppy and Other Deadly Plants" cover (by Leonard Baskin)
Paper samples from the Stevens-Nelson Paper Corporation
Gehenna financial records and other correspondence
Invoices, mostly unrelated to the arts

Writings by others: "To Rebecca, Growing Up" by Fannie Stearns Gifford, [1922]; Private publishing of "To Rebecca, Growing Up," n.d.; Letters between F.W. Main and Atlantic Monthly concerning the private publication of "To Rebecca, Growing Up," 1923; Christmas cards from Frederick Wilson Main, [1924-1936]
Gehenna Press: "Aesopics," [1968] and Cranwell Chapel commission, n.d.
Gehenna Press: "The Matchmaker's Lament and Other Astonishments"
Sealed papers of Esther Baskin: sealed until 2025
Correspondence [from sealed papers of Esther Baskin?]
Gehenna Press: "Timothy Trace/Fifty Rare and Unusual Books"
Gehenna Press: "Romeyn de Hooghe to the Burgermasters of Harlem"
Letters re: Aristotle's "Politics" and "Poetics" which Baskin did not agree to illustrate
Materials re: an exhibition at Bowdoin college
Designs for a book on journalism by Joseph Pulitzer, Jr.
Gehenna Press: "Between Wars and Other Poems" by Anne Haley
Gehenna Press: "A Listing of Books to Be Had from The Gehenna Press Several New Works Now in Press"
Gehenna Press: "West-Ostlicher Divan: Goethe's West-East Divan"
Materials and correspondence re: book "Baskin: Sculpture, Drawings, Prints"
Gehenna Press: "Euripides' Hippolytus" and large proofs
Anchor book covers, n.d.; Gehenna Press exhibition posters, n.d. and Vienna prints, [1800s]
Vienna pritnts - Kleiner, [1800s] and Gehenna Press, "Titus Andronicus," n.d.

Miscellaneous large prints, n.d.; A Passover Hagadah: reviews and comments, 1974; Newspaper articles re: Prof. Thomas Cornell, 1969; Title page of Hosie's Aviary, 1978; Announcement for the Gehenna Shakespeare, removed from Box 2, n.d.; Proof of the Gehenna Press's "Alcun: A Dialogue," removed from Box 34, n.d.; Proof of the Gehenna Press's "Peter," removed from Box 50, 1971; Proof of the Gehenna Press's "The Drawings of Jacob de Gheyn II," removed from Box 63, n.d. and Proof of the Gehenna Shakespeare's "Titus Andronicus," removed from Box 91, 1969
Gehenna Press: "Titus Andronicus" proof; Vienna prints and original [Vienna?] pencil drawings
Vienna prints; random Gehenna material and photographs of works
Random Gehenna material; manuscript: Othello and unidentified material
Gehenna [covers?], "John Woolman's Dream of the Fox and the Cat" [oversize portfolio]
Baskin, Leonard - Autograph notes of his remarks at the memorial gathering for Harold Hugo in the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript
Folders of unused printed sheets for Hippolytus
"The Wages of Sin are Death," woodcut print
"Pig is Forbidden to Thee," woodcut print

Printed material [on shelf], including: 1) works containing contributions from multiple authors, including Leonard Baskin; 2) works about Leonard Baskin; 3) Gehenna Press publications; 4) works about Gehenna Press; 5) other works by Leonard Baskin (not Gehenna)
